question: According to the docs, summarize: Built as a personal project to explore production grade RAG architecture patterns beyond naive "embed and retrieve" pipe...
contexts: Built as a personal project to explore production grade RAG architecture patterns beyond naive "embed and retrieve" pipelines.

question: What problem does this address: Instead of a single embed → retrieve → generate step, the pipeline adds three layers of reasoning to improve accuracy on...?
contexts: Instead of a single embed → retrieve → generate step, the pipeline adds three layers of reasoning to improve accuracy on legal text, where precision (exact article numbers, exact wording) matters far more than in general Q&A:

question: In one sentence, what does the project say about: Why this matters: a plain RAG setup often retrieves loosely related chunks and answers confidently anyway. This pipeline...?
contexts: Why this matters: a plain RAG setup often retrieves loosely related chunks and answers confidently anyway. This pipeline explicitly checks retrieval quality (Self RAG) and rejects out of scope questions (Router) before generating an answer, reducing hallucinated legal citations.

question: In one sentence, what does the project say about: Scores each answer 1–5 using an LLM judge against ground truth article citations, and prints an average score...?
contexts: Scores each answer 1–5 using an LLM judge against ground truth article citations, and prints an average score.

question: According to the docs, summarize: A 10 question test set covering both easy (direct retrieval) and harder (multi hop / less obvious phrasing) legal questi...
contexts: A 10 question test set covering both easy (direct retrieval) and harder (multi hop / less obvious phrasing) legal questions is scored automatically using an LLM as judge rubric (1 = wrong, 5 = correct with exact article citation).
